1	# CampusFit Static Delivery Acceptance and Architecture Audit
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: `Fail`
5	
6	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
7	- What was reviewed: repository documentation, manifests, environment templates, OpenAPI contract, Vue frontend source under `repo/apps/web`, Spring Boot backend source under `repo/services/api`, Flyway migrations, and committed tests under both app layers.
8	- What was not reviewed: runtime behavior, browser rendering, Docker/container startup, database connectivity, actual MySQL execution, external channels (email/SMS/WeCom), or generated artifacts.
9	- What was intentionally not executed: project startup, `docker`, tests, builds, migrations, and external services, per audit boundary.
10	- Claims requiring manual verification: actual startup/build behavior, real browser UX, MySQL partition behavior, slow-query screen population, encrypted export download/decryption, and any connected-environment delivery channels.
11	
12	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
13	- Prompt core goal: offline-first local system for three roles with local auth, fitness assessment/goals/check-ins, study planning/review/export, inbound receiving workflow, admin master data import/merge/history, notifications, security controls, exports/account deletion, and performance features.
14	- Main implementation areas mapped: auth/security (`com.campusfit.auth`, `shared.security`), fitness (`com.campusfit.fitness`), study (`com.campusfit.study`), inbound (`com.campusfit.inbound`), master data (`com.campusfit.masterdata`), notifications (`com.campusfit.notification`), export/deletion (`com.campusfit.export_`), reporting (`com.campusfit.reporting`), frontend route modules under `repo/apps/web/src/modules`, and committed tests/contracts/docs.
15	
16	## 4. Section-by-section Review
17	
18	### 1. Hard Gates
19	
20	#### 1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
21	- Conclusion: `Partial Pass`
22	- Rationale: startup/config/test instructions, env template, and module READMEs exist, but the static verification path is unreliable because the declared frontend strict build contract is internally broken and multiple tests/contracts are stale against current source.
23	- Evidence: `repo/README.md:24-107`, `repo/.env.example:6-30`, `repo/services/api/README.md:56-79`, `repo/apps/web/package.json:8-14`, `repo/apps/web/tsconfig.json:6`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:7-23`, `repo/contracts/openapi.yaml:328-341`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:32-36`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalServiceTest.java:37`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:95-117`
24	- Manual verification note: build/test claims require execution; static evidence is insufficient to accept them as working.
25	
26	#### 1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt
27	- Conclusion: `Fail`
28	- Rationale: the repository is organized around the requested domains, but major prompt-critical flows are weakened or contradicted in the delivered implementation: weight-goal semantics are wrong, account migration is incomplete, masked exports are not honored, inbound nested actions do not respect parent receipt boundaries, and active admin/operations UI routes are stubbed or wired to incompatible contracts.
29	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:42-67`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:87-90`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:74-78`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:141-162`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:78-118`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingListPage.vue:35-41`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:27-42`
30	
31	### 2. Delivery Completeness
32	
33	#### 2.1 Full coverage of core prompt requirements
34	- Conclusion: `Fail`
35	- Rationale: some core requirements are present in part, but the delivery misses or materially weakens full coverage for secure metric storage, weight-goal behavior, full cross-account migration, masked exports, admin merge/import round-trip, notification read-receipt visibility, and prompt-wide pagination/performance expectations.
36	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java:43-72`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/CheckIn.java:37-45`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:145-158`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyExportImportService.java:71-134`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:34`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataExportController.java:49-58`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:120-127`, `repo/services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:56-58`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/GoalController.java:32-36`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:48-52`
37	
38	#### 2.2 Basic end-to-end deliverable vs partial/demo delivery
39	- Conclusion: `Fail`
40	- Rationale: the repository has full-stack structure, but the active routed frontend contains placeholder actions and contract drift that prevent acceptance as a coherent end-to-end deliverable rather than a partially wired implementation.
41	- Evidence: `repo/README.md:5-14`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/PutawayPage.vue:13-19`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue:77-79`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/DiscrepanciesPage.vue:26-28`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:27-42`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:204-211`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataMergeController.java:42-55`
42	
43	### 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
44	
45	#### 3.1 Engineering structure and module decomposition
46	- Conclusion: `Partial Pass`
47	- Rationale: the backend is sensibly decomposed by domain, with separate services, policies, controllers, and migrations; the frontend is also modularized by feature. That said, the frontend and verification assets have drifted enough that module boundaries no longer guarantee consistent contracts.
48	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/README.md:16-29`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/policy/ReferentialIntegrityPolicy.java:9-57`, `repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts:4-158`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:8-108`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:7-23`
49	
50	#### 3.2 Maintainability and extensibility
51	- Conclusion: `Partial Pass`
52	- Rationale: there is evidence of extensible design in policies, adapters, cache/reporting services, and state-machine logic, but maintainability is significantly reduced by stale tests/specs, duplicated incompatible contracts, and placeholder routed features.
53	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/policy/EffectiveDatePolicy.java:11-18`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/AggregationService.java:41-85`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:326-385`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:204-211`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/DuplicateMergeServiceTest.java:44-46`, `repo/contracts/openapi.yaml:527-562`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyExportController.java:20-31`
54	
55	### 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism
56	
57	#### 4.1 Error handling, logging, validation, and API design
58	- Conclusion: `Fail`
59	- Rationale: there is basic validation and exception handling, but the delivery still contains material engineering defects: raw identifiers appear in logs, goal/check-in metrics remain plaintext at rest, export/import contracts are inconsistent, and several API/UI contracts are mismatched.
60	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:64-98`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:89-115`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:86-102`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:97`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:58-64`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/CheckIn.java:37-45`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/components/NotificationItem.vue:40`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:272-281`
61	
62	#### 4.2 Real product/service shape vs example/demo
63	- Conclusion: `Fail`
64	- Rationale: the backend has product-like structure, but the shipped active frontend still contains multiple "coming soon", toast-only, or mock-like paths in core routed modules, which is not consistent with a production-shaped delivery for the prompt scope.
65	- Evidence: `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingListPage.vue:38-41`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/PutawayPage.vue:17-19`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:27-42`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/DiscrepanciesPage.vue:26-28`
66	
67	### 5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
68	
69	#### 5.1 Business-goal understanding and fit
70	- Conclusion: `Fail`
71	- Rationale: the repository clearly understands the requested domains, but several prompt semantics are implemented incorrectly: weight-loss progress logic uses the wrong direction, export/import does not support full account migration, deletion/log/export masking requirements are not fully honored, and master data round-trip/import expectations are broken.
72	- Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:43-50`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:185-192`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:88-90`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:145-158`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyExportImportService.java:71-134`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:236-242`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataExportController.java:55-57`
73	
74	### 6. Aesthetics
75	
76	#### 6.1 Frontend visual and interaction quality
77	- Conclusion: `Cannot Confirm Statistically`
78	- Rationale: static source shows intentional layout primitives, spacing, hover states, and visual hierarchy, but runtime visual correctness cannot be accepted statically, especially because the active frontend has unresolved contract/type drift.
79	- Evidence: `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingListPage.vue:90-136`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/components/NotificationItem.vue:47-127`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/pages/NotificationsPage.vue:82-111`, `repo/apps/web/package.json:8`, `repo/apps/web/tsconfig.json:6`
80	- Manual verification note: browser rendering, route navigation, and responsive behavior require manual verification after the frontend is made build-consistent.
81	
82	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
83	
84	### Blocker
85	1. Active frontend violates its own strict build/type contract
86	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
87	   - Evidence: `repo/apps/web/package.json:8`, `repo/apps/web/tsconfig.json:6`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:7-23`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useDiscrepancies.ts:7-28`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:68-108`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue:23-24`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue:91-93`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue:116-146`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/components/NotificationItem.vue:40`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:172-187`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/api-adapter.interface.ts:272-281`
88	   - Impact: the delivered frontend cannot be accepted as statically buildable/verifiable; active routed pages and composables disagree on store shape, route names, and DTO fields.
89	   - Minimum actionable fix: reconcile routed pages/composables with current store and DTO contracts, then run the declared `vue-tsc --noEmit` gate before acceptance.
90	
91	### High
92	2. Weight-goal baseline and achievement semantics are incorrect
93	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
94	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/AssessmentService.java:38-44`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:43-50`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:185-192`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:87-90`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalServiceTest.java:57-58`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalServiceTest.java:116`
95	   - Impact: weight-loss/weight-gain goals can start from the wrong baseline and can be marked achieved using the wrong comparison direction, breaking a core prompt flow.
96	   - Minimum actionable fix: resolve baselines from encrypted assessment metrics and apply goal-type-aware progress/achievement logic, with explicit tests for loss and gain goals.
97	
98	3. Sensitive fitness metrics are still persisted in plaintext for goals and check-ins
99	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
100	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V12__encrypt_goal_checkin_metrics.sql:1-3`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/Goal.java:43-72`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/entity/CheckIn.java:37-45`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:53-67`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:58-64`
101	   - Impact: explicit prompt requirement for encrypted personal metrics at rest is not met for all fitness data.
102	   - Minimum actionable fix: remove or null out plaintext metric columns for active writes, migrate remaining data, and verify only encrypted columns remain authoritative.
103	
104	4. Inbound nested actions ignore the parent receipt boundary
105	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
106	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:78-118`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundLineService.java:54-78`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:49-63`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java:84-97`
107	   - Impact: an authorized operations user can act on a line/task/discrepancy unrelated to the receipt ID in the URL, which is an object-level authorization/business-boundary defect.
108	   - Minimum actionable fix: require service-layer verification that the nested resource belongs to the receipt path parameter before processing.
109	
110	5. Core operations and admin UI flows are incomplete or wired to wrong contracts
111	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
112	   - Evidence: `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingListPage.vue:31-41`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/PutawayPage.vue:13-19`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue:77-79`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/DiscrepanciesPage.vue:26-28`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:27-42`, `repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts:65-68`, `repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts:79-82`, `repo/apps/web/src/app/router.ts:99-102`, `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:204-211`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataMergeController.java:42-55`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/dto/MergeRequest.java:16-23`
113	   - Impact: receiving creation, putaway completion, discrepancy resolution, and master-data create/edit/delete/merge cannot be accepted as delivered end-to-end user flows.
114	   - Minimum actionable fix: replace toast-only placeholders with real adapter/store actions and align route names and merge request/query shapes with backend contracts.
115	
116	6. Export/import does not satisfy full account migration or default masking requirements
117	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
118	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:141-162`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentResponse.java:17-31`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:74-78`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyExportImportService.java:71-134`
119	   - Impact: exports include raw account identifiers and raw fitness metrics, while import only restores study data, so the prompt’s cross-account migration and masked-export behavior are not delivered.
120	   - Minimum actionable fix: define a full account export/import schema, mask sensitive fields by default where required, and implement import handlers for account/fitness payloads.
[result-id: r1]

120	   - Minimum actionable fix: define a full account export/import schema, mask sensitive fields by default where required, and implement import handlers for account/fitness payloads.
121	
122	7. Master-data export/import round trip is broken by incompatible date formats
123	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
124	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:34`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:236-242`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataExportController.java:55-57`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/MasterDataExportController.java:91-95`
125	   - Impact: system-generated master-data exports cannot be re-imported without manual date rewriting, breaking the admin import/export workflow.
126	   - Minimum actionable fix: standardize import/export dates to one format and enforce it in both CSV and Excel paths.
127	
128	8. Tests, docs, and OpenAPI are stale enough to undermine acceptance verification
129	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
130	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalServiceTest.java:37`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:33-39`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:35-36`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:33-39`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/DuplicateMergeServiceTest.java:44-46`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/DuplicateMergeService.java:23-30`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:95-117`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/dto/ExportResponse.java:17-25`, `repo/services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationServiceTest.java:114-121`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:104-116`, `repo/contracts/openapi.yaml:328-341`, `repo/contracts/openapi.yaml:527-562`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:32-36`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyExportController.java:20-31`
131	   - Impact: a reviewer cannot trust the repository’s own tests/spec/docs as a coherent verification path without first repairing them.
132	   - Minimum actionable fix: update or remove stale tests/specs, then keep contract and test suites enforced in CI against current source.
133	
134	### Medium
135	9. Pagination and scalability requirements are only partially implemented
136	   - Conclusion: `Partial Pass`
137	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:56-58`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:25-31`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/GoalController.java:32-36`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:32-36`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:48-52`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/TermController.java:32-35`, `repo/services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V3__create_fitness_tables.sql:84-97`
138	   - Impact: prompt-level scalability claims are overstated; most core list endpoints ignore page/size despite a shared pagination config.
139	   - Minimum actionable fix: move core list endpoints to a shared paginated response contract and add tests for page/size behavior.
140	
141	10. Notification delivery status/read-receipt delivery is only partially exposed
142	   - Conclusion: `Partial Pass`
143	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:90-99`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:120-127`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/entity/NotificationTarget.java:32-37`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/store.ts:19-56`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/pages/NotificationsPage.vue:12-79`, `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/api.ts:15-16`
144	   - Impact: users can mark items read and admins can fetch delivery records, but the prompt’s "read receipts and delivery status visible to authorized users" is not delivered as a complete UI/API flow.
145	   - Minimum actionable fix: expose read-receipt status in the admin status API and add routed UI for authorized delivery/read tracking.
146	
147	11. Sensitive identifiers are still logged unmasked in some security-relevant flows
148	   - Conclusion: `Fail`
149	   - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:86-88`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:98-102`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:97`
150	   - Impact: the prompt requires masking sensitive fields in logs by default, but export/deletion logs still print raw user identifiers.
151	   - Minimum actionable fix: remove raw identifier logging and standardize masked logging helpers across export/deletion/security-sensitive services.
152	
153	## 6. Security Review Summary
154	- Authentication entry points: `Pass`
155	  - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:28-58`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/PasswordService.java:12-21`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptService.java:27-59`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/security/SessionAuthenticationFilter.java:56-93`
156	  - Reasoning: sign-up/sign-in/sign-out/current-user flows exist, passwords use bcrypt, failed-attempt lockout is implemented, and bearer sessions are checked with inactivity timeout/sliding refresh.
157	- Route-level authorization: `Pass`
158	  - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:48-57`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:60-65`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:34-52`
159	  - Reasoning: coarse route protection for `/api/admin/**`, `/api/inbound/**`, and authenticated `/api/**` is present, with additional `@PreAuthorize` on sensitive admin endpoints.
160	- Object-level authorization: `Fail`
161	  - Evidence: positive checks in `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:80-106`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyPlanService.java:40-46`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:104-116`; failure path in `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:78-118`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundLineService.java:54-78`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:49-63`
162	  - Reasoning: many user-owned resources enforce ownership, but inbound nested operations do not validate that nested IDs belong to the parent receipt.
163	- Function-level authorization: `Partial Pass`
164	  - Evidence: `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:60-65`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:34-52`, `repo/services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:110-118`
165	  - Reasoning: some sensitive functions use method-level role checks, but coverage is not uniform and still depends on flawed object-boundary handling in inbound flows.
166	- Tenant / user isolation: `Partial Pass`

## BUILD/TYPE CONTRACT VIOLATIONS REPORT

### Summary of Key Mismatches

Your frontend is running with **strict TypeScript mode enabled** (`strict: true` in tsconfig.json) and a **pre-build type-check** (`vue-tsc --noEmit && vite build`), yet multiple components violate the API contract defined in your interfaces.

---

### VIOLATION 1: NotificationItem.vue - Missing Required Field

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/modules/notifications/components/NotificationItem.vue`

**Issue:** Line 28 accesses `notification.id` but the Discrepancy interface (and potentially conflicting uses) defines different required fields than what's being used.

**DTO Definition (api-adapter.interface.ts, lines 289-298):**
```typescript
export interface Notification {
  id: number
  notificationId: number  // ← REDUNDANT/DUPLICATE ID
  type: string
  title: string
  body?: string
  read: boolean
  readAt?: string
  createdAt: string
}
```

**Component Usage (NotificationItem.vue, line 28):**
```typescript
@click="emit('click', notification.id)"
```

**Problem:** The Notification DTO has **both** `id` and `notificationId` fields. The component uses `notification.id`, but the contract is unclear which is the source of truth. This dual-ID pattern indicates a schema mismatch or incomplete DTO definition.

---

### VIOLATION 2: useDiscrepancies.ts - Accessing Undefined Field

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useDiscrepancies.ts`

**Issue:** Lines 16, 18, and 22 access fields that don't match the strict Discrepancy type definition.

**Code (useDiscrepancies.ts, lines 15-23):**
```typescript
if (filterResolved.value === 'resolved') {
  result = result.filter(d => d.resolved)  // ← Line 16
} else if (filterResolved.value === 'unresolved') {
  result = result.filter(d => !d.resolved)  // ← Line 18
}

if (filterSupervisor.value) {
  result = result.filter(d => d.supervisorRequired)  // ← Line 22
}
```

**DTO Definition (api-adapter.interface.ts, lines 226-241):**
```typescript
export interface Discrepancy {
  id: number
  receiptId: number
  lineId: number
  discrepancyType: 'QUANTITY' | 'QUALITY' | 'WRONG_ITEM'
  expectedValue: number
  actualValue: number
  variancePercent: number
  reasonCode?: string
  supervisorRequired: boolean  // ✓ This exists
  resolvedBy?: number
  resolvedAt?: string
  notes?: string
  createdAt: string
  resolved: boolean  // ✓ This exists
}
```

**Problem:** Actually, these fields **do exist** in the DTO, so this composable is correct. However, the store (store.ts, lines 60-63) **transforms** the data:

```typescript
const payload = res.data as any
if (Array.isArray(payload)) {
  discrepancies.value = payload.map((d: any) => ({
    ...d,
    resolved: !!d.resolvedBy,  // ← Recalculated from resolvedBy
  }))
}
```

This is a transformation mismatch - the API returns `resolvedBy`, but the store creates a derived `resolved` field. This works but is not type-safe.

---

### VIOLATION 3: store.ts - Unsafe Type Casting

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts`

**Issue:** Multiple locations use `as any` type assertions, breaking strict mode guarantees.

**Lines 24, 58:**
```typescript
const payload = res.data as any  // Line 24
const payload = res.data as any  // Line 58
```

**Problem:** With `strict: true`, using `as any` defeats type safety and violates the build contract. The API responses should be typed correctly against the DTO interfaces.

---

### VIOLATION 4: ReceivingDetailPage.vue - Status Field Type Mismatch

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/pages/ReceivingDetailPage.vue`

**Issue:** Lines 33, 37-38, 42-43, 47-48 access and manipulate `receipt.value.status` with string operations.

**Code (ReceivingDetailPage.vue):**
```typescript
return getAvailableTransitions(receipt.value.status)  // Line 33
const s = receipt.value?.status?.toLowerCase()  // Line 37
```

**DTO Definition (api-adapter.interface.ts, line 178):**
```typescript
export interface ReceivingReceipt {
  ...
  status: ReceiptStatus  // ← Strong enum type
  ...
}
```

**Type Definition (ReceiptStatus is imported from api-adapter.interface):**
The `ReceiptStatus` type is a union type (not shown in your excerpt, but referenced). Calling `.toLowerCase()` on a status that may already be uppercase indicates the component assumes string values but the DTO is stricter.

---

### VIOLATION 5: useReceiptDetail.ts - Missing Null Safety

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts`

**Issue:** Line 16 casts the string parameter as `ReceiptStatus` without validation.

**Code (useReceiptDetail.ts, lines 15-16):**
```typescript
async function transition(status: string, notes?: string) {
  await store.transitionReceipt(receiptId, status as ReceiptStatus, notes)
```

**Problem:** The function accepts a `string` but casts it to `ReceiptStatus`. With strict mode, this should validate the enum at runtime or define the parameter as `ReceiptStatus` directly.

---

### VIOLATION 6: http-adapter.ts - Incomplete Type Definition

**File:** `repo/apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts` (lines 190-220)

The `exportItems` method (line 217-220) returns `Promise<ApiResponse<Blob>>` but the implementation doesn't validate the response type. With strict mode, the response should be properly typed.

---

### ROOT CAUSE

Your audit is correct. The mismatches stem from:

1. **`as any` assertions** in store.ts that bypass strict type checking
2. **Dual-field confusion** in Notification DTO (id vs notificationId)
3. **Data transformations** (resolved field) not reflected in types
4. **String type assumptions** when the DTO uses stronger types (ReceiptStatus)
5. **Missing runtime validation** for enum-like values

### RECOMMENDED FIXES

1. Remove all `as any` casts and properly type API responses
2. Clarify the Notification schema (choose id OR notificationId, not both)
3. Adjust Discrepancy typing to account for the `resolved` computed field
4. Type status parameters as `ReceiptStatus`, not `string`
5. Add runtime enum validators for API payloads

5	export function useDiscrepancies() {
6	  const store = useOperationsStore()
7	  const { discrepancies: allDiscrepancies, loading, error } = storeToRefs(store)
8	
9	  const filterResolved = ref<'all' | 'resolved' | 'unresolved'>('all')
10	  const filterSupervisor = ref(false)
11	
12	  const filteredDiscrepancies = computed(() => {
13	    let result = allDiscrepancies.value
14	
15	    if (filterResolved.value === 'resolved') {
16	      result = result.filter(d => d.resolved)
17	    } else if (filterResolved.value === 'unresolved') {
18	      result = result.filter(d => !d.resolved)
19	    }
20	
21	    if (filterSupervisor.value) {
22	      result = result.filter(d => d.supervisorRequired)
23	    }
24	
25	    return result
26	  })
27	
28	  return {
29	    discrepancies: filteredDiscrepancies,
30	    allDiscrepancies,
31	    loading,
32	    error,
33	    filterResolved,
34	    filterSupervisor,
35	  }
36	}

### 2. **OpsDashboard.vue**
- Props: `DashboardData` interface
- Shows 4 stat cards for operations (Active Receipts, Pending Discrepancies, Putaway Queue, Completed Today)
- Displays receipt status overview with status indicators
- Accesses arrays via optional chaining for safety

### 3. **UserDashboard.vue**
- Props: `DashboardData` interface
- 4 stat cards: Fitness Goals, Study Streak, Check-Ins, Notifications
- Goal progress section with dynamic progress bars
- Recent activity display

### 4. **useAssessment.ts**
- Composable for fitness assessment form with 3 steps
- Manages form state with validation logic
- Methods: `validateStep1()`, `validateStep2()`, `nextStep()`, `prevStep()`, `submit()`
- Uses Pinia store (`useFitnessStore`)
- Returns form, step management, and submission state

### 5. **store.ts** (Fitness)
- Pinia store (`useFitnessStore`)
- State: assessment, goals, currentGoal, checkIns, loading, error, totalGoals
- Methods for CRUD operations: fetch/save assessment, fetch/create goals, create check-ins
- Uses `fitnessApi()` for API calls
- Error handling with `normalizeError()`

### 6. **AuditTimeline.vue**
- Props: `entries` array of `ChangeHistoryEntry`
- Displays change history in timeline format
- Shows entity type, changed by, and detailed field changes
- No mutation/emit patterns

### 7. **ImportPreview.vue**
- Props: `ImportResult` interface
- Summary stats: total rows, success count, error count
- Error display with row numbers and messages
- Emits: `confirm` and `cancel` events

### 8. **useMerge.ts**
- Composable for merge operations
- Manages entity type selection
- Methods: `fetchCandidates()`, `merge(sourceId, targetId)`
- Uses Pinia store (`useMasterDataStore`)
- Returns refs and methods

### 9. **DiscrepancyCard.vue**
- Props: `Discrepancy` interface
- Displays discrepancy with type, item name, description
- Shows resolution status with conditional badges
- Includes logic for supervisor requirement detection

### 10. **InspectionForm.vue**
- Props: `items` array of `ReceiptItem`, `loading` boolean
- Form for inspection with pass/fail per item
- `passAll()` button functionality
- Emits `submit` event with overall result and notes

### 11. **PutawayItem.vue**
- Props: `task` of type `PutawayTask`
- Shows task details: name, status, quantity, location
- Input field for actual location confirmation
- Emits `confirm` event with task ID and location

### 12. **ReceivingLineItems.vue**
- Props: `items` array, `editable` boolean
- Table display of receipt items with expected/received quantities
- Discrepancy detection and highlighting
- Editable input for received quantities
- Emits `updateQty` event

### 13. **ReceivingTable.vue**
- Props: `receipts` array of `ReceivingReceipt`, `loading` boolean
- Uses `DataTable` component
- 6 columns: receipt number, type, supplier, status, created date, actions
- `toTableData()` transformation function
- Emits `rowClick` event

### 14. **usePutaway.ts**
- Composable managing putaway operations
- Computed properties: `pendingTasks`, `completedTasks`
- Returns store refs and computed values

### 15. **TopBar.vue**
- Header component with page title from route meta
- User menu with profile and sign-out options
- Custom `v-click-outside` directive implementation
- Notification icon link
- User avatar with initial letter

### 16. **useFormState.ts**
- Generic form state management composable
- Type-safe form handling with validation
- State: values, errors, touched, isSubmitting, isDirty
- Methods: setField, setError, validate, submit, reset
- Validation rule execution and error management

### 17. **useSignUp.ts**
- Authentication composable for signup
- Form state management with username, email, password fields
- Custom password match validation
- Server error handling
- Integration with Pinia auth store

### 18. **setup.ts** (__tests__)
- Vitest test setup file
- Cleanup after each test: Vue component cleanup and localStorage clearing
- Minimal configuration

---

All files are now available for review and type error fixes. The files are primarily Vue 3 Composition API with TypeScript, using Pinia for state management and contain various form, data display, and dashboard components.

## Summary of Re-review Fixes

| Issue | What was partial | What was done to complete it |
|-------|-----------------|------------------------------|
| **#3** Encryption backfill | `BigDecimalAesEncryptor` had no plaintext fallback — legacy rows like `"200.00"` would crash on decrypt | Added try-parse-as-BigDecimal-first fallback in `convertToEntityAttribute`. If the stored value is a plain number, it's used directly; only Base64 encrypted values go through decryption. |
| **#6** Account export/import | Import had no old→new goal ID remap, check-ins referenced stale IDs. No profile metadata export/restore. | `AccountImportService` now builds a `goalIdRemap` HashMap during goal import and uses it when importing check-ins. Export adds `profileData` (username, email, phone). Import restores email/phone via `restoreProfile()`. |
| **#8** Tests & OpenAPI drift | OpenAPI still said `ApiResponseListStudyPlanResponse` for study plans (now Page). No tests for import remap or admin delivery status. | OpenAPI updated to `PageResponseStudyPlanResponse` with page/size params. Added `AccountImportServiceTest` (goal ID remap + profile restore). Added `getDeliveryStatus_returnsReadReceiptsForTargets` test in `NotificationServiceTest`. |
| **#9** Pagination incomplete | School, Major, Course, Class controllers still returned raw `List<?>` | All 4 controllers now accept `page`/`size` params and return `Page<MasterDataResponse>`, matching the pattern already used by TermController. |

1	# CampusFit Static Audit
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: Fail
5	- Basis: The repository has real product structure, documentation, security primitives, and domain modules, but several explicit prompt requirements are either missing end-to-end or materially weakened by contract, workflow, and authorization defects.
6	
7	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
8	- Reviewed:
9	  - `README.md`, `.env.example`, `docker-compose.yml`, `contracts/openapi.yaml`
10	  - Vue router, pages, stores, composables, adapters, and selected frontend tests under `apps/web/src`
11	  - Spring controllers, services, entities, security config, migrations, and selected tests under `services/api/src`
12	- Not reviewed:
13	  - Runtime DB contents
14	  - Browser-rendered UI behavior
15	  - External email/SMS/WeCom integrations
16	  - Real file download/upload interoperability
17	- Intentionally not executed:
18	  - Project startup
19	  - Docker / Docker Compose
20	  - Tests
21	  - Browser/manual flows
22	- Manual verification required for:
23	  - Runtime boot and environment wiring
24	  - Actual MySQL partition creation and query performance
25	  - Scheduled cleanup/aggregation timing
26	  - Real browser rendering / responsive behavior
27	  - External notification channels
28	  - Export-file download, decrypt, upload, and import interoperability
29	
30	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
31	- Core prompt: offline-first local deployment with three roles, local auth, fitness assessment/goals/check-ins, study plans tied to academic hierarchy, inbound receiving state machine with discrepancy/supervisor rules, admin master-data import/export/merge/history, unified notification center, protected exports/account deletion, and performance/admin reporting.
32	- Main implementation areas mapped:
33	  - Frontend routes/pages/stores/adapters in `apps/web/src/app` and `apps/web/src/modules/*`
34	  - Backend controllers/services/security/migrations in `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/*` and `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration`
35	  - Static contract/documentation in `contracts/openapi.yaml`, `README.md`, `.env.example`, and `docker-compose.yml`
36	
37	## 4. Section-by-section Review
38	
39	### 1. Hard Gates
40	- `1.1 Documentation and static verifiability`
41	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
42	  - Rationale: Startup/configuration artifacts are present and structurally consistent enough for a reviewer to find entry points, but the shipped OpenAPI contract is materially inconsistent with implemented controllers and real frontend expectations.
43	  - Evidence: `README.md:5`, `README.md:24`, `.env.example:6`, `.env.example:28`, `docker-compose.yml:3`, `services/api/pom.xml:26`, `apps/web/package.json:6`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:4`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:241`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1222`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1249`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/CheckInController.java:34`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:50`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:86`
44	  - Manual verification note: Runtime startup still requires manual verification because no commands were executed.
45	- `1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt`
46	  - Conclusion: Fail
47	  - Rationale: Several explicit prompt commitments are not met end-to-end: study plans are not tied to the full academic hierarchy, supervisor review is not privilege-separated, dashboard data contracts do not match, and encrypted export files do not have a matching import flow.
48	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue:16`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/types.ts:43`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:27`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V2__seed_roles_permissions.sql:6`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/dto/DashboardResponse.java:18`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/UserDashboard.vue:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`
49	
50	### 2. Delivery Completeness
51	- `2.1 Whether all explicitly stated core functional requirements are implemented`
52	  - Conclusion: Fail
53	  - Rationale: Core flows are incomplete or broken in static review: discrepancy retrieval is missing from the delivered API, the dashboard contract is incompatible across layers, cross-account migration from an exported file is absent, and the study-plan hierarchy is only partially modeled.
54	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:171`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:43`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/DashboardService.java:57`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/OpsDashboard.vue:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:53`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:64`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V4__create_study_tables.sql:5`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/pages/PlansPage.vue:69`
55	- `2.2 Whether the project is a basic 0-to-1 deliverable rather than a partial/demo`
56	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
57	  - Rationale: The repository is a real multi-module application with docs, migrations, routes, security, and tests. Mock behavior is explicitly gated, but several real HTTP paths depend on mock-only assumptions or missing backend support.
58	  - Evidence: `README.md:42`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/adapter-factory.ts:7`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/mock-adapter.ts:337`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:166`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:29`
59	
60	### 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
61	- `3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition`
62	  - Conclusion: Pass
63	  - Rationale: Frontend and backend responsibilities are separated by domain and role; security, reporting, export, inbound, study, and fitness concerns are not collapsed into a single file.
64	  - Evidence: `README.md:5`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:21`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/DashboardService.java:22`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:30`
65	- `3.2 Whether the project shows maintainability and extensibility`
66	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
67	  - Rationale: DTO/service layering and targeted policies exist, but repeated contract drift between frontend/backend/OpenAPI and repeated in-memory pagination patterns increase change risk.
68	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/policy/ReferentialIntegrityPolicy.java:9`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicy.java:18`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:51`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:36`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:276`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/CheckInController.java:34`
69	
70	### 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism
71	- `4.1 Whether the engineering details reflect professional software practice`
72	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
73	  - Rationale: The codebase has centralized exception handling, input validation, RBAC, AES-based converters for fitness metrics, caching, and scheduled maintenance. That said, key API contracts drift, supervisor review is under-protected, account exports are not masked by default, and account deletion semantics do not match the stated requirement.
74	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:22`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentRequest.java:18`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/EncryptionService.java:14`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:48`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:188`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:80`
75	- `4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service`
76	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
77	  - Rationale: The application has role-targeted pages, admin tooling, scheduled maintenance, and domain services, but the current test/mocking strategy allows severe real-integration defects to remain undetected.
78	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/admin/pages/PerformancePage.vue:15`, `apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MergePage.vue:37`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/CampusFitApplication.java:7`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:32`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:20`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/__tests__/ReceivingDetailPage.spec.ts:16`
79	
80	### 5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
81	- `5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal and constraints`
82	  - Conclusion: Fail
83	  - Rationale: The repository understands the broad product shape, but several prompt semantics are weakened: supervisor review is not a distinct approval boundary, migration is not file-based end-to-end, and the study hierarchy is reduced to optional term/course IDs with no school/major/class linkage in the user flow.
84	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V2__seed_roles_permissions.sql:6`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue:35`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyPlanRequest.java:15`

86	### 6. Aesthetics
87	- `6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario`
88	  - Conclusion: Cannot Confirm Statistically
89	  - Rationale: The source contains responsive layouts, card-based grouping, visual hierarchy, and interaction styling, but actual rendering quality, alignment, spacing, and feedback were not executed in a browser.
90	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/UserDashboard.vue:14`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/AdminDashboard.vue:13`, `apps/web/src/modules/admin/pages/PerformancePage.vue:68`, `apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:68`

93	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
94	
95	1. Severity: Blocker
96	   Title: Cross-account migration from an exported file is not implemented end-to-end
97	   Conclusion: Fail
98	   Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:225`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:53`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:64`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:285`
99	   Impact: The delivered export flow writes password-encrypted `.enc` files, but the import path accepts raw JSON maps rather than uploaded/exported files, and there is no user-facing import flow. The prompt's cross-account migration requirement is therefore absent in practice.
100	   Minimum actionable fix: Add a matching file-upload/decrypt/import path for exported account files and expose it in the exports UI; verify the exported artifact can round-trip through import without manual JSON extraction.
101	
102	2. Severity: High
103	   Title: Discrepancy retrieval required by the web client is missing from the delivered API
104	   Conclusion: Fail
105	   Evidence: `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:171`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:56`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:12`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:43`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:570`
106	   Impact: Operations pages depend on `GET /api/inbound/receipts/{id}/discrepancies`, but no such controller route or contract path exists. Real discrepancy pages cannot load against the delivered backend.
107	   Minimum actionable fix: Implement and document a discrepancy list endpoint, or refactor the frontend to use an existing backend path and add controller tests for it.
108	
109	3. Severity: High
110	   Title: PUTAWAY task reads are implemented as task creation and can duplicate tasks
111	   Conclusion: Fail
112	   Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:103`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:13`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:108`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:29`
113	   Impact: The frontend treats `POST /putaway` without `taskId` as a read operation, but the backend generates and saves new tasks on that path. Simple page refreshes or background loads can multiply pending tasks and corrupt workflow state.
114	   Minimum actionable fix: Separate read and create/complete semantics. Add a dedicated `GET /putaway` endpoint and make task generation idempotent.

180	    Title: Account deletion password confirmation is collected in the UI but ignored by the actual deletion flow
181	    Conclusion: Partial Fail
182	    Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/exports/components/AccountDeletionDialog.vue:16`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:51`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/store.ts:33`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:80`
183	    Impact: The UI implies re-authentication, but neither the frontend store nor the backend endpoint consumes the password. A valid session alone is enough to trigger deletion.
184	    Minimum actionable fix: Either remove the misleading password prompt or enforce password re-verification server-side before processing deletion.
185	
186	## 6. Security Review Summary
187	- Authentication entry points
188	  - Conclusion: Pass
189	  - Evidence and reasoning: Local sign-up/sign-in/sign-out/current-user routes exist; passwords are bcrypt-hashed; failed-attempt lockout and 30-minute inactivity timeout are implemented in code and config. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:28`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/PasswordService.java:12`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthService.java:82`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptService.java:19`, `services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:45`.
190	- Route-level authorization
191	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
192	  - Evidence and reasoning: Backend route guards exist for `/api/admin/**`, `/api/inbound/**`, and authenticated `/api/**`; frontend routes also carry `requiresAuth` and role metadata. However, the supervisor-review route is broader than the prompt implies. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:48`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:169`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`.
193	- Object-level authorization
194	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
195	  - Evidence and reasoning: Service-layer ownership checks exist for study plans, goals, check-ins, notifications, and exports. Static implementation is present, but controller-level negative-path coverage is weak. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyPlanService.java:40`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:90`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:36`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:104`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:128`.
196	- Function-level authorization
197	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
198	  - Evidence and reasoning: Method-level `@PreAuthorize` is used for admin-only notification creation/status, admin performance, and admin role assignment. The weakness is that supervisor review is not protected by a distinct approval authority. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:34`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingController.java:38`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:60`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`.
199	- Tenant / user data isolation
200	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
201	  - Evidence and reasoning: User-scoped reads and ownership checks are present in notifications, exports, study, and fitness flows, but static test coverage is insufficient to prove all HTTP-level isolation paths. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:80`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:129`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyPlanService.java:44`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:109`.
202	- Admin / internal / debug endpoint protection
203	  - Conclusion: Pass
204	  - Evidence and reasoning: Admin endpoints are behind backend role checks, and the explicitly exposed health endpoint is narrowly scoped. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:49`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingController.java:38`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:80`.
205	
206	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
207	- Unit tests
208	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
209	  - Rationale: There is meaningful service/policy coverage for auth, fitness, inbound state rules, master-data integrity/merge, notifications, exports, and deletion/import logic.
210	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:60`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:31`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:37`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:66`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/DuplicateMergeServiceTest.java:52`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationServiceTest.java:51`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:68`
211	- API / integration tests
212	  - Conclusion: Fail
213	  - Rationale: Controller-level coverage is limited to auth and reporting. Core HTTP flows for inbound, study, master-data, exports, notifications, and dashboard payload compatibility are largely untested.
214	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:44`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:35`
215	- Logging categories / observability
216	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
217	  - Rationale: Structured logging exists for exception handling, exports, import jobs, encryption setup, file retention, and performance aggregation. Admin performance reporting is also present.
218	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:84`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:99`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:119`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:45`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/AggregationService.java:41`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/SlowQueryLogService.java:27`
219	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses
220	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
221	  - Rationale: User IDs are masked in export logs, and fitness metrics are encrypted at rest, but raw export file paths are logged and other high-risk privacy behavior exists elsewhere (for example unmasked account export content).
222	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/FieldMasker.java:8`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:99`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:45`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/EncryptionService.java:14`
223	
224	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
225	
226	### 8.1 Test Overview
227	- Unit tests exist for backend services/policies across auth, fitness, inbound, master data, notifications, and exports. Evidence: `services/api/pom.xml:104`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:35`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:23`
228	- API/controller tests exist, but only for auth and reporting. Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:44`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:35`
229	- Frontend tests exist and use Vitest. Evidence: `apps/web/package.json:10`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:1`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/__tests__/ReceivingDetailPage.spec.ts:1`
230	- Documentation provides test commands, but they were not executed for this audit. Evidence: `README.md:60`, `apps/web/package.json:10`
231	
232	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
233	
234	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
235	| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
236	| Local auth, lockout, sign-in/out, session validation | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:53`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:60`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:31` | Hashed password asserted at `AuthServiceTest.java:85`; token issuance at `AuthServiceTest.java:137`; lockout timing at `LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:50` | sufficient | No controller test for admin role assignment | Add controller tests for role assignment and negative authorization cases |
237	| Fitness goal recalculation and audit trail after missed check-ins | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:37`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInServiceTest.java:81` | Audit record asserted at `GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:103`; date/target changes asserted at `GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:57` | basically covered | `CheckInServiceTest` does not actually assert the recalculation call/path | Add a service test that proves two missed check-ins trigger `recalculationPolicy.recalculate(...)` |
238	| Inbound state machine and discrepancy thresholds | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:66`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:58` | Transition guards asserted at `InboundStateMachineTest.java:149`; supervisor-required discrepancy asserted at `DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:133` | basically covered | No controller test for real HTTP workflow, missing discrepancy endpoint, or putaway duplication bug | Add controller/integration tests for receipt detail, discrepancy retrieval, putaway task reads, and supervisor review |
239	| Supervisor review authorization separation | None found | None found | missing | Current tests do not verify that only a distinct supervisor/admin authority can approve threshold discrepancies | Add security tests proving ordinary operations staff cannot perform supervisor review |
240	| Study plans tied to academic hierarchy | None found | None found | missing | No tests assert school/major/class/course linkage because the UI and request model do not implement it | Add frontend and backend tests for hierarchy selection, validation, and persistence |
241	| Export password protection and account import | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:68`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportServiceTest.java:43` | Password requirement asserted at `ExportServiceTest.java:76`; goal remap asserted at `AccountImportServiceTest.java:79` | insufficient | Tests cover raw service maps, not encrypted file round-trips or UI flows | Add end-to-end tests for download/upload/decrypt/import of exported account files |
242	| Account deletion semantics | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:52` | Test explicitly accepts `DELETED` status and retained user row at `AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:163` | insufficient | The tests lock in non-compliant anonymization behavior instead of the prompt's hard-delete requirement | Replace with tests that assert identifiers are removed or detached from retained audit artifacts |
243	| Dashboard API and role-specific rendering | `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:57` | The page test manually injects fake store data like `{ goals: 3 }` at `DashboardPage.spec.ts:64` and stubs role dashboards at `DashboardPage.spec.ts:39` | insufficient | No test exercises the real backend payload shape, so the shipped mismatch is invisible to the suite | Add contract tests for backend payloads and frontend rendering against actual `DashboardResponse` schemas |
244	| Notification delivery/read receipts | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationServiceTest.java:51`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/policy/DeliveryChannelPolicyTest.java:37` | Read receipt assertions at `NotificationServiceTest.java:159`; offline default channel asserted at `DeliveryChannelPolicyTest.java:38` | basically covered | No controller-level admin authorization tests for create/status routes | Add controller tests for `401/403` on notification create and status endpoints |
245	| Object-level authorization on user-owned resources | None meaningful at controller level | None meaningful at controller level | missing | Severe cross-user defects in study, goal, export, or notification HTTP flows could still pass the test suite | Add negative controller tests for `403/404` on foreign IDs across study, fitness, export, and notification endpoints |
246	
247	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
248	- Authentication
249	  - Conclusion: basically covered
250	  - Evidence and reasoning: Auth controller/service/lockout tests cover sign-up, sign-in, invalid credentials, lockout behavior, and authenticated `/api/me`. See `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:53`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:118`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:50`.
251	- Route authorization
252	  - Conclusion: insufficient
253	  - Evidence and reasoning: Only dashboard/performance authorization is controller-tested. There is no comparable coverage for inbound, notifications admin routes, exports, or master-data admin routes. See `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:80`.
254	- Object-level authorization
255	  - Conclusion: insufficient
256	  - Evidence and reasoning: Ownership checks exist in services, but there are no controller tests showing foreign-resource access is denied for study, fitness, or export APIs.
257	- Tenant / data isolation
258	  - Conclusion: insufficient
259	  - Evidence and reasoning: Notification service tests prove user-scoped fetch in service code (`NotificationServiceTest.java:84`), but there is no end-to-end coverage for cross-user isolation at HTTP boundaries.
260	- Admin / internal protection
261	  - Conclusion: insufficient
262	  - Evidence and reasoning: Admin-only reporting is covered, but notification creation/status and user-role assignment are not similarly tested. Severe authorization regressions could still pass.
263	
264	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
265	- Fail
266	- Major risks covered: authentication basics, lockout logic, some fitness recalculation policy behavior, inbound state-machine transitions, discrepancy threshold calculations, notification delivery/read receipt service logic, export password requirement, and deletion anonymization behavior.
267	- Major risks not covered: real frontend/backend contract compatibility, missing discrepancy endpoint, putaway task duplication bug, supervisor review privilege separation, study hierarchy linkage, controller-level object authorization, admin-route breadth, and exported-file round-trip migration. The existing tests could all pass while those severe defects remain present.
268	
269	## 9. Final Notes
270	- This audit is strictly static. No runtime behavior, Docker startup, browser rendering, or test execution was performed.
271	- The repository is not a toy sample; it contains substantial implementation. The acceptance failure comes from prompt-critical gaps and weakened controls, not from missing scaffolding.
272	- The highest-value remediation path is:
273	  - reconcile shared contracts first (frontend, backend, OpenAPI),
274	  - repair the broken core flows (discrepancies, putaway reads, dashboard payloads, file-based migration),
275	  - then tighten authorization semantics (supervisor review, deletion re-auth, prompt-compliant account deletion),
276	  - and finally add controller/integration coverage for those exact paths.
277

16	const emit = defineEmits<{
17	  submit: [data: { title: string; description?: string; courseId?: number; termId?: number }]
18	}>()
19	
20	const title = ref('')
21	const description = ref('')
22	
23	const errors = ref<Record<string, string>>({})
24	
25	const isValid = computed(() => {
26	  return title.value.trim().length > 0
27	})
28	
29	function validate(): boolean {
30	  errors.value = {}
31	  if (!title.value.trim()) errors.value.title = 'Title is required'
32	  return Object.keys(errors.value).length === 0
33	}
34	
35	function handleSubmit() {
36	  if (!validate()) return
37	  emit('submit', {
38	    title: title.value,
39	    description: description.value || undefined,
40	  })
41	}
42	</script>
